decks.notes: The deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures and disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of creature removal, counter spells, and bounce effects. The creatures in the deck have powerful abilities that can generate advantage and pressure the opponent, while the spells provide additional value and control. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through a combination of tempo advantage and card advantage.

This control deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly using UrzaTron lands to play powerful Eldrazi creatures and devastating colorless spells. It aims to control the game through disruption with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Kozilek's Command, while also providing threats with creatures like Sire of Seven Deaths and Glaring Fleshraker. Karn, the Great Creator acts as a versatile tool to shut down opponent's artifacts, while Ugin, Eye of the Storms can sweep the board clean. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with big threats and powerful control elements.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays while using burn spells and combat tricks to control the battlefield. With cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge to deal direct damage, along with efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to pressure the opponent, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly. The inclusion of cards like Blood Moon and Static Prison also disrupts the opponent's game plan, giving the deck the upper hand in a fast-paced match.

The deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent using low-cost spells and creature abilities. It aims to control the board with aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, while also utilizing burn spells like Lightning Bolt to finish off the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Slickshot Show-Off and Violent Urge to further pressure the opponent and maintain tempo. With a mix of efficient creatures, burn spells, and card advantage through cards like Mishra's Bauble and Expressive Iteration, the deck aims to win the game as quickly as possible.

This deck focuses on a fast and aggressive playstyle, using a combination of efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and powerful removal spells like Prismatic Ending. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with creatures that can quickly deal damage. The inclusion of cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Consign to Memory allows for additional disruption and control over the game. The deck's strategy revolves around creating an early advantage and maintaining pressure to secure a victory.

This deck focuses on aggressive play with disruptive elements to control the opponent's board state while applying pressure. It utilizes powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to quickly deal damage to the opponent. Consign to Memory and Solitude provide removal options to deal with threats, while Quantum Riddler and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker offer card advantage. The deck also includes cards like Teferi, Time Raveler for additional disruption and Ephemerate for value plays. Overall, the deck aims to maintain tempo and disrupt the opponent's plans while applying pressure to secure a victory.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, then bringing them back onto the battlefield using cards like Goryo's Vengeance. The deck also has control elements like Force of Negation and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, cards like Faithful Mending and Ephemerate help dig for combo pieces and protect key cards. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces swiftly and reanimate powerful threats to overwhelm the opponent before they can respond effectively.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble a powerful landless combo utilizing cards like Goblin Charbelcher and Lotus Bloom. By controlling the opponent's spells and disrupting their game plan with cards like Force of Negation and Disrupting Shoal, the deck aims to set up a winning board state with Thundertrap Trainer or Tameshi, Reality Architect. The inclusion of counterspells and card draw engines like Sea Gate Restoration and Waterlogged Teachings help the deck maintain tempo and consistency in executing its game-winning combo.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to quickly cheat big creatures like Griselbrand and Ulamog onto the battlefield. Supporting cards like Ephemerate and Solitude provide additional control and protection. The deck also includes a variety of lands to help fix mana and enable the combo pieces. A key part of the strategy is to take advantage of the graveyard as a resource, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming the opponent with powerful threats.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and burn spells to quickly overpower opponents. It utilizes powerful cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to deal damage quickly, while also including control elements like Voice of Victory and Thraben Charm to disrupt opponents' strategies. The deck aims to maintain a strong board presence and apply pressure early in the game, forcing opponents to react defensively or risk being overwhelmed by the aggressive threats it presents.

This deck focuses on assembling the "UrzaTron" combo of Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant, and Urza's Tower to generate a large amount of mana. It then utilizes powerful Eldrazi creatures and artifacts like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms to control the game and overwhelm the opponent. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Trinisphere and Thought-Knot Seer while deploying massive threats like Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger to quickly close out the game.

This deck focuses on quickly and efficiently reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Ulamog, the Defiler using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to protect the combo pieces. Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog provide card advantage and filtering, while Emperor of Bones can provide additional reanimation targets. Overall, the deck aims to bring out big threats early and overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their own game plan.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and burn spells. It aims to put pressure on the opponent early in the game, using cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to deal damage quickly. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to ensure victory. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Thraben Charm provide additional reach and flexibility in dealing with opposing threats. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around dealing damage efficiently and decisively.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures, then bringing them back onto the battlefield for a devastating combo. By using cards like Faithless Looting, Thought Scour, and Unearth, the player can quickly set up their graveyard with creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones. Once the graveyard is set up, Persist can bring back these creatures for another round of attacks. With the right combination of spells and creatures, this deck can overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures from the graveyard.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck includes powerful creatures like Otawara, Soaring City and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares to apply pressure while maintaining control. With spells like Fatal Push, Force of Negation, and Subtlety, the deck can handle a variety of threats efficiently. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Psychic Frog provide card advantage and selection, while cards like Cling to Dust and Sheoldred's Edict help disrupt the opponent's graveyard strategies.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by limiting the opponent's options and maximizing card advantage. It utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Narset, Teferi, and powerful spells like Orim's Chant and Galvanic Discharge to disrupt the opponent's game plan while also drawing cards and digging for key pieces with Consult the Star Charts. The deck also includes versatile removal options like Prismatic Ending and board wipes like Wrath of the Skies to maintain control of the battlefield. With a mix of counterspells, removal, and card advantage, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents to secure victory.
